# NeMo Gym

> NeMo Gym is the open environment and rollout layer for training and evaluating agentic models. It turns benchmarks, tools, sandboxes, and reward functions into scalable RL-ready environments.

NeMo Gym is part of the NVIDIA NeMo platform. It separates an agentic environment into four components — dataset, agent harness, model, and reward verifier — that are independently varied. The same environment that scores a model produces training rollouts, which is the bridge competing eval-only frameworks don't have.

## About

What NeMo Gym is, where it fits in the NVIDIA NeMo platform, and the core concepts behind RL-ready environments.

- [Overview](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/about): Motivation, what NeMo Gym solves, and how the pieces fit together.
- [Ecosystem](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/about/ecosystem): Where NeMo Gym fits in the NVIDIA NeMo platform, how it compares to external RL and eval frameworks, and which tools to pair it with.
- [Concepts](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/about/concepts): Mental model behind building RL training environments — when to use RL over SFT, how environment components work together, and how verification signals drive learning.
- [Architecture](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/about/concepts/architecture): How NeMo Gym separates dataset, agent harness, model, and reward into four independently-varied components.
- [Configuration System](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/about/concepts/configuration): How YAML configuration files define Model, Resources, and Agent servers as modular blocks.
- [Environment Components](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/about/concepts/core-components): The Model, Resources, and Agent server building blocks that compose a NeMo Gym environment.
- [Reward Semantics](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/about/concepts/reward-semantics): What NeMo Gym does on verifier errors, timeouts, retries, multiple rollouts per task, pass@1 vs pass@k, and aggregation.
- [Task Verification](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/about/concepts/task-verification): What task verification is and how rewards drive model training.
- [Training Approaches](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/about/concepts/training-approaches): The differences between SFT, DPO, and GRPO, and why RLVR puts the focus on environments.
- [Release Notes](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/about/release-notes): What's new, what's fixed, and what's deprecated in each NeMo Gym release.
- [Migration Guide 0.2 → 0.3](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/about/release-notes/migration-guide): What's changing in 0.3.0 and how to update existing 0.2.x setups.

## Get Started

Install NeMo Gym, configure your model, and run a first benchmark.

- [Get Started](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/get-started): Install NeMo Gym, run your first benchmark, and build your first environment.
- [Quickstart](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/get-started/quickstart): Install NeMo Gym, configure your model, and score a Blackjack agent end-to-end in about 10 minutes.
- [Prerequisites](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/get-started/prerequisites): System requirements for running NeMo Gym.
- [Installation](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/get-started/installation): Install NeMo Gym from source, PyPI, or a container image.
- [Configuration](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/get-started/configuration): Set up env.yaml with model credentials before running NeMo Gym.
- [Create Your First Environment](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/get-started/create-first-environment): Build a single-decision Blackjack environment by subclassing GymnasiumServer.
- [Choose an Agent](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/get-started/choose-an-agent): Pick the right agent harness for your task: simple, proof-refinement, verifiers integration, or a custom external harness.

## Build Environments

Patterns and end-to-end guides for authoring new training and evaluation environments.

- [Overview](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments): Build custom environments for training or evaluation using NeMo Gym.
- [Supported Patterns](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/supported-patterns): Pick the right shape (single-step, multi-step, stateful) before you start coding.
- [Single-Step Environment](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/supported-patterns/single-step): Build a single-step environment where the model produces one answer that the verifier scores.
- [Multi-Step Environment](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/supported-patterns/multi-step): Build a multi-step environment where the agent loops over model and tool calls until the task is complete.
- [Stateful Environment](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/supported-patterns/stateful): Build an environment whose tools maintain per-rollout state via session cookies.
- [Real-World Environment](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/real-world-environment): End-to-end walkthrough of converting a real-world task (Workplace Assistant) into a NeMo Gym environment.
- [Generating Training Data](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/real-world-environment/generating-training-data): Generate the JSONL training data for a real-world environment by sampling from your task source.
- [Resources Server Implementation](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/real-world-environment/resources-server-implementation): Implement the resources server for the real-world Workplace Assistant environment.
- [Integrations](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/integrations): Integration patterns for third-party benchmark libraries and adding new benchmarks.
- [Add a Benchmark](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/integrations/add-a-benchmark): Steps and best practices for adding a high-fidelity benchmark to NeMo Gym.
- [Integrate External Libraries](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/integrations/external-libraries): Wrap an existing third-party benchmark or environment library at the agent server level.
- [Verification](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/verification): How verification logic plugs into NeMo Gym — `verify()` returns reward, `compute_metrics()` aggregates across rollouts.
- [Aggregate Metrics](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/verification/aggregate-metrics): How NeMo Gym computes aggregate metrics for each agent via the /aggregate_metrics endpoint after rollout collection.
- [LLM-as-a-judge in Verification](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/verification/llm-as-judge): Use an LLM as the judge for verifying open-ended outputs that don't have a single deterministic answer.
- [Author Workflow](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/author-workflow): Workflow for environment authors — quality checklist, profiling, contribution.
- [Environment Quality Checklist](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/author-workflow/quality-checklist): What every official NeMo Gym environment must satisfy — deterministic verification, no oracle leakage, locked dependencies, validation command, expected baseline variance.
- [Profile a Resources Server](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/author-workflow/profile): Use profiling_enabled to measure verifier performance under high concurrency.
- [Contribute](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/author-workflow/contribute): Contribute new environments, RL framework integrations, documentation, and bug fixes.
- [Design a Customer Evaluation](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/designing-customer-evaluation): Guiding principles for designing an evaluation against a customer's real workflow.
- [Data](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/data): How NeMo Gym datasets use JSONL format and connect agent servers to resources servers.
- [Prepare and Validate](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/data/prepare-validate): Format and validate JSONL datasets for NeMo Gym training using ng_prepare_data.
- [Download from Hugging Face](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/data/download-huggingface): Download JSONL datasets from Hugging Face Hub for NeMo Gym training.
- [Prompt Config](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/build-environments/data/prompt-config): Apply YAML-based prompt templates at rollout time to enable prompt sweeps without re-preparing JSONL data.

## Evaluate Models

Score, compare, and inspect agent behavior on existing benchmarks or your own customer evals.

- [Evaluate Models](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/evaluate): Score, compare, and inspect agent behavior on existing benchmarks or your own customer evals.
- [Run a Benchmark](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/evaluate/run/run-a-benchmark): End-to-end — pick a benchmark from the catalog, configure your model, score it.
- [Validate Your Setup](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/evaluate/run/validate-your-setup): Confirm your NeMo Gym install + model credentials produce expected results before running real evals.
- [Compare Agents Across Benchmarks](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/evaluate/compare/compare-agents): Run the same benchmark with two or more agent harnesses, then compare per-task pass rates side by side.
- [Score a Model Against Customer Data](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/evaluate/compare/score-customer-data): Run an existing benchmark's scoring logic over your own JSONL dataset for production validation.
- [Profile Rewards](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/evaluate/analyze/profile-rewards): Compute per-task pass rates from a rollouts JSONL — pass@1, pass@k, success threshold.
- [Inspect Failures](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/evaluate/analyze/inspect-failures): Where to find rollout JSONL, model responses, verifier outputs, and server logs after a failed eval.
- [Benchmark Catalog](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/evaluate/benchmark-catalog): Every shipped NeMo Gym environment grouped by domain (coding, math, reasoning, agentic) with verified-flag status and config paths.

## Train Models

Hands-on tutorials for running RL training with NeMo Gym environments across supported frameworks.

- [Overview](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/train-models): Hands-on tutorials with supported training frameworks.
- [NeMo RL](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/train-models/nemo-rl-grpo): Train Nemotron Nano 9B v2 to improve multi-step tool-calling using GRPO on the Workplace Assistant environment.
- [Setup](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/train-models/nemo-rl-grpo/setup): Set up the host environment, dependencies, and credentials needed to run the NeMo RL GRPO tutorial.
- [About Workplace Assistant](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/train-models/nemo-rl-grpo/about-workplace-assistant): Overview of the Workplace Assistant environment used in the NeMo RL GRPO tutorial.
- [Gym Configuration](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/train-models/nemo-rl-grpo/gym-configuration): Configure the NeMo Gym side of the NeMo RL GRPO training run.
- [NeMo RL Configuration](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/train-models/nemo-rl-grpo/nemo-rl-configuration): Configure the NeMo RL trainer for the GRPO Workplace Assistant run.
- [Single Node Training](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/train-models/nemo-rl-grpo/single-node-training): Launch a single-node NeMo RL GRPO training job against a NeMo Gym environment.
- [Multi-Node Training](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/train-models/nemo-rl-grpo/multi-node-training): Scale the NeMo RL GRPO training run across multiple nodes.
- [Unsloth](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/train-models/unsloth): Fine-tune models with Unsloth using NeMo Gym environments.
- [Multi-Environment Training](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/train-models/multi-environment-training): Train on multiple NeMo Gym environments simultaneously (multi-verifier training).
- [Offline Training (SFT/DPO)](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/train-models/offline-training-w-rollouts): Use collected rollouts to do offline training (SFT/DPO) outside of the RL loop. Experimental.
- [Model Recipes](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/train-models/recipes): Tutorials for running Gym with the data recipes used to train shipped models.
- [Nemotron 3 Nano](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/train-models/recipes/nemotron-3-nano): Complete setup for distributed training of Nemotron 3 Nano 30B across multiple nodes using Slurm and Ray.
- [Nemotron 3 Super](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/train-models/recipes/nemotron-3-super): Pointer to the NeMo RL recipe for training Nemotron 3 Super with NeMo Gym.

## Reference

Quick lookup for configuration, server APIs, deployment topology, and compatibility matrices.

- [Reference](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/reference): Quick lookup for configuration syntax, options, and specifications.
- [Configuration](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/reference/configuration): Complete syntax and field specifications for NeMo Gym configuration files.
- [Environment Variables](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/reference/configuration/environment-variables): Environment variables NeMo Gym reads at startup or runtime.
- [Ray and Distributed](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/reference/configuration/ray-and-distributed): Ray cluster integration, distributed execution, and operational gotchas.
- [Compatibility](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/reference/compatibility): Provider, framework, and sandbox compatibility matrices.
- [Provider Compatibility](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/reference/compatibility/providers): Tested model and provider matrix — vLLM, Azure/OpenAI, OpenAI-compatible endpoints, Responses vs chat completions, tool calling, reasoning blocks.
- [RL Framework Compatibility](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/reference/compatibility/rl-frameworks): NeMo Gym version compatibility with supported RL training frameworks (NeMo RL, TRL, Unsloth).
- [Sandbox & Execution Matrix](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/reference/compatibility/sandbox-execution): Where rollouts run — local, Ray, Docker, cloud sandboxes. Network behavior, dependency installation, timeout semantics, security boundaries per backend.
- [Servers](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/reference/servers): Reference for the agent, model, and resources server types.
- [Agent Server](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/reference/servers/agent): The agent harness — orchestrates the model + tool-call loop and routes the final output to the verifier.
- [Model Server](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/reference/servers/model): Stateless LLM inference endpoints that receive a conversation and return the model's next output.
- [vLLM Model](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/reference/servers/model/vllm): The VLLMModel server wrapping vLLM's Chat Completions endpoint and converting it to the OpenAI Responses API schema.
- [Resources Server](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/reference/servers/resources): The "world" the agent interacts with — defines the task, the tools and actions, and the verification logic that returns reward.
- [RL Framework Integration](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/reference/rl-framework-integration): Reference for integrating NeMo Gym into a new RL training framework.
- [Generation Backend](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/reference/rl-framework-integration/generation-backend-and-openai-compatible-http-server): Requirements for the OpenAI-compatible HTTP server NeMo Gym calls during training.
- [Integration Footprint](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/reference/rl-framework-integration/gym-integration-footprint-and-form-factor): Components required to integrate Gym into your training framework, with links to NeMo RL reference implementations.
- [Success Criteria](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/reference/rl-framework-integration/gym-rl-framework-integration-success-criteria): Validation criteria for confirming a Gym integration works correctly.
- [On-Policy Corrections](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/reference/rl-framework-integration/openai-compatible-http-server-on-policy-correction): Issues with using an OpenAI-compatible HTTP server in multi-step and multi-turn RL training.
- [Deployment Topology](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/reference/deployment-topology): Training framework deployment topology and how NeMo Gym servers connect to RL trainers.
- [FAQ](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/reference/faq): Quick answers to commonly asked NeMo Gym questions.

## Troubleshooting

Symptom-driven index of common failure modes, configuration errors, and known footguns.

- [Troubleshooting](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/troubleshooting): Solutions for common errors and issues.
- [Footguns](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/troubleshooting/footguns): Symptom-driven index of NeMo Gym gotchas — Hydra quoting, vLLM context length, Ray socket paths, and other non-obvious failure modes.
- [Configuration Errors](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/troubleshooting/configuration): Errors that appear when running ng_run or ng_collect_rollouts.

## Resources

Glossary, development setup, and supplementary lookup material.

- [Resources](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/resources): Glossary, development setup, and supplementary lookup material.
- [Glossary](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/resources/glossary): Definitions of NeMo Gym terms — training environment, agent harness, resources server, rollout, verifier, RLVR, and more.
- [Development Setup](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/latest/resources/development-setup): Set up your development environment for NeMo Gym contributions.

## API and CLI

Python API and CLI command reference (separate tab from the documentation pages).

- [API Reference](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/api/python): Auto-generated Python API reference for the `nemo_gym` package.
- [CLI Overview](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/api/cli): Overview of the `ng_*` command surface, grouped by category.
- [Servers](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/api/cli/servers): `ng_run`, `ng_test`, `ng_test_all`, `ng_dev_test`, `ng_init_resources_server`, `ng_status`.
- [Rollouts](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/api/cli/rollouts): `ng_collect_rollouts`, `ng_e2e_collect_rollouts`, `ng_reward_profile`.
- [Data](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/api/cli/data): `ng_prepare_data` plus GitLab and HuggingFace dataset registry commands.
- [Diagnostics](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/api/cli/diagnostics): `ng_dump_config`, `ng_help`, `ng_version`, `ng_pip_list`, `ng_reinstall`.

## Blog

Engineering notes and design rationale.

- [Blog](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/blog): Engineering notes, infrastructure decisions, and design rationale.
- [aiohttp vs httpx](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/blog/aiohttp-vs-httpx): Why NeMo Gym uses aiohttp — httpx is O(n²) in queued requests and stalls under heavy concurrency.
- [Responses API Evolution](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/blog/responses-api-evolution): Why NeMo Gym uses the OpenAI Responses API as its native schema and how it differs from Chat Completions.
- [SWE-RL Case Study](https://docs.nvidia.com/nemo/gym/blog/swe-rl-case-study): Case study (Feb 2026) covering the SWE-RL environment design and lessons learned.
